whyZeroEn.title:
Build for free.
Pay us when you win.
whyZeroEn.subtitle: We take 10% equity. You bring the idea. That's the entire deal.

pillars.title: Zero upfront cost
pillars.desc: We build your full-stack MVP — Next.js, Supabase, Vercel — for $0. No retainers, no hourly billing.

pillars.title: Real equity partnership
pillars.desc: We take 10% via SAFE note. We only win when you win. Aligned incentives from day one.

pillars.title: Selective by design
pillars.desc: We accept fewer than 20% of applications. Apply now while slots are open.

whyZeroEn.urgency: Currently reviewing applications. Spots are limited.

points.title: Mutual NDA before you apply
points.desc: We sign a confidentiality agreement with every applicant before you share a single detail.

points.title: Aligned incentives
points.desc: We take equity. If we stole ideas instead of building them, we'd make $0.

points.title: Full data control
points.desc: Request deletion of your data at any time. Rejected applications are wiped within 30 days.

tools.desc: Global edge deployment, preview URLs, analytics. Zero DevOps.

tools.name: Stripe

apply.title: Apply to ZeroEn
apply.subtitle: Tell us about your idea. We'll build it for free.

step0.title: Before you share your idea
step0.subtitle: We take your trust seriously. Here's our commitment:
points: We will not share, copy, or use your idea without written agreement
This is mutual — we're bound by the same terms
You can request deletion of all your data at any time
Rejected applications are wiped within 30 days
step0.checkboxLabel: I've read and agree to the mutual confidentiality agreement

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: From idea to launch — nine steps, zero upfront cost.

discover.name: DISCOVER
discover.desc: We find you through our build-in-public content, Twitter/X posts, Instagram, and developer communities. Or you find us directly at zeroen.dev.

apply.name: APPLY
apply.desc: Submit a detailed application on zeroen.dev. Tell us about your idea, target market, and background. Takes about 10 minutes.
apply.badge: Protected by our mutual NDA — signed before you share anything

score.name: SCORE
score.desc: We score every application on 4 dimensions: Viability (is this a real problem?), Commitment (are you serious?), Feasibility (can we build it in MVP scope?), Market (is there real demand?). 15+ out of 20 to accept.

onboard.name: ONBOARD
onboard.desc: If accepted, you fill a detailed questionnaire and join a kickoff call. We lock the scope together. Scope is frozen at this point — no feature creep.

build.name: BUILD
build.desc: Development begins. We build your full-stack MVP using Next.js + Supabase. Regular updates in your founder dashboard.

launch.name: LAUNCH
launch.desc: We deploy to Vercel. You get 30 days of free support post-launch. You run the staging review and give final sign-off.

operate.name: OPERATE
operate.desc: After launch, ongoing hosting, monthly fixes, and analytics reporting keep your app running and growing.

grow.name: GROW
grow.desc: New features beyond the original scope are available as add-ons, quoted upfront per request. Your revenue share kicks in.

upsell.name: UPSELL
upsell.desc: Monthly analytics surface opportunities. WebMori audit service available. We grow with you.

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.subtitle: Apply in 10 minutes. We respond within 3 business days.

terms.title:
The deal,
in plain English.
terms.subtitle: Standard terms for every ZeroEn client. No surprises.

items.label: Equity
items.value: 10% via SAFE note. Converts when you incorporate.

items.label: Revenue share
items.value: ~10% of app revenue, negotiated per deal.

items.label: Platform fee
items.value: $50/mo after your MVP launches. Covers hosting + 1 fix/mo.

items.label: Scope freeze
items.value: MVP scope locked at kickoff. New features = per-request charge.

items.label: Kill switch
items.value: 90 days unpaid → agreement terminates. We keep the code.

items.label: Portfolio rights
items.value: We always retain the right to showcase this work.

sections.title: 1. Equity Agreement
sections.body: ZeroEn receives 10% equity via a Simple Agreement for Future Equity (SAFE note). The SAFE converts to equity shares upon the client's incorporation or qualifying financing event. If the client never incorporates, a profit-sharing arrangement of approximately 10% of net revenue applies instead. ZeroEn retains an anti-dilution floor: a minimum equity percentage is maintained if the client raises external funding.

sections.title: 2. Revenue Share
sections.body: A revenue share of approximately 10% of the client's app revenue is negotiated at kickoff and documented in the individual agreement. Revenue share activates once the client's application generates revenue. The exact percentage is flexible per deal and is fixed in writing before the build begins.

sections.title: 3. Platform Fee
sections.body: After the MVP launches, a platform fee of USD $50 per month applies. This covers: hosting on ZeroEn's Vercel Pro account, one small fix or minor change per month (at ZeroEn's discretion), and a monthly analytics PDF report. The platform fee begins 30 days after launch. Payment is via Stripe subscription. The client's application remains live only while the platform fee is paid.

sections.title: 4. Scope Freeze
sections.body: The MVP scope is agreed and locked at the kickoff call. This scope is documented and signed by both parties. Any features, changes, or additions beyond the agreed scope are treated as per-request charges. ZeroEn is not obligated to build out-of-scope work. Per-request charges are quoted upfront and must be paid before work begins.

sections.title: 5. Per-Request Charges
sections.body: Work beyond the locked MVP scope is available as per-request charges: Small changes (1–4 hours) USD $50–100; Medium features (1–3 days) USD $200–500; Large builds (1–2 weeks) USD $500–2,000. All quotes are provided upfront. Work begins only after payment is received.

sections.title: 6. Kill Switch
sections.body: If the platform fee remains unpaid for 90 consecutive days, the agreement terminates automatically. Upon termination: the client's hosted application is taken offline, ZeroEn retains full rights to the codebase, and all equity and revenue share arrangements are voided. ZeroEn will make reasonable efforts to notify the client before reaching the 90-day threshold.

sections.title: 7. Reversion Clause
sections.body: If the client does not launch their application within 6 months of the agreed build completion date, full code rights revert to ZeroEn. The client may request an extension in writing. Extensions are granted at ZeroEn's discretion.

sections.title: 8. IP Ownership
sections.body: Intellectual property is shared proportionally to equity stake. ZeroEn holds 10% IP rights corresponding to its equity. The client holds the remaining rights. ZeroEn always retains the right to use the work in its portfolio and marketing materials, including screenshots, case studies, and build logs.

sections.title: 9. Founder Vesting
sections.body: The client's equity interest vests over time. If the client abandons the project (defined as no communication or active use for 6+ months), their equity stake reduces proportionally. This clause protects ZeroEn from building for founders who disengage.

sections.title: 10. Governing Law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of Canada (Ontario). Any disputes are resolved first through good-faith negotiation, then through binding arbitration if necessary. ZeroEn and the client agree to resolve disputes without litigation where possible.

sections.title: 11. Confidentiality & Non-Disclosure
sections.body:
Both ZeroEn and the client agree to keep each other's confidential information private. Confidential information includes: application data, business plans, technical specifications, financial projections, user research, and any other information shared during the application, onboarding, or build process.

Neither party will share, copy, or use the other's confidential information without written consent. Standard exclusions apply: information that is publicly available, independently developed, or legally required to be disclosed is not covered by this clause.

Data deletion: any applicant — accepted or rejected — may request deletion of all their submitted data at any time. Rejected application data is automatically deleted within 30 days of the rejection decision. Requests can be sent to the operator directly.

This confidentiality obligation survives the termination of any agreement between ZeroEn and the client. Governing law for this clause follows Section 10.
